fixApproach: The language-tier split from the parent issue (#21) removes Swift from supported languages (it has no structural analyzer). This fix: (1) strips Swift-specific production code — the swiftStandardPolicy constant and its case in languagePolicy(), plus the testdata YAML file; (2) updates existing tests that still iterate over Swift as a supported language or count swift-standard as a policy pack; (3) adds a new language_tiers_test.go to lock in the analyzer-registry contract; (4) adds N/A-grade and unsupported-banner expectations to report tests so unsupported-language units are explicitly tested rather than accidentally passing with misleading F grades. Production changes in certifier.go and the report layer ensure unsupported-language records receive N/A (not score-0→F) and are excluded from aggregate scoring. No new features or Swift analysis tier are introduced.
openQuestions: Should testdata/policies/swift-standard.yml be deleted outright, or kept but excluded by the policy loader? Preferred: delete.
How should N/A grade be represented in the domain model — a new Grade constant (e.g. GradeNA) in internal/domain/dimension.go, or a string sentinel in the report layer only?
What is the exact format of the unsupported banner in report cards — is it already designed/specified somewhere, or does it need to be designed as part of this work?
Does the policy pack loader (internal/config) need to filter packs by supported-language list, or is removing the YAML file sufficient?
steps: Remove swiftStandardPolicy constant and `case "swift"` from languagePolicy() in cmd/certify/init_cmd.go
Update cmd/certify/cli_test.go: remove "swift" from TestLanguagePolicy_AllSupported slice
Update internal/config/policy_test.go: change pack count 7→6, remove "swift-standard" from expected-name list
Ensure internal/engine/certifier.go marks units in unsupported languages with N/A grade (not score-0→F)
Ensure internal/report/card.go renders an unsupported banner when unsupported-language units are present
Ensure internal/report/full.go excludes unsupported-language records from aggregate score calculations (buildLanguageDetail)
Create internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go: test ForLanguage returns nil for swift, non-nil for go/ts/js/py/rs, and certifier yields N/A for swift units
Add N/A-grade and unsupported-banner test cases to internal/report/card_test.go
Add unsupported-exclusion test cases to internal/report/full_test.go
Run go build -o build/bin/certify ./cmd/certify/ to confirm compilation
Run go test ./... -count=1 and iterate until all tests pass
summary: Fix test regressions from language-tier split: remove Swift from supported-languages lists, update policy-pack counts, create language_tiers_test.go, and add N/A-grade expectations to report tests.
uiStateNotes: Report card text gains an "unsupported" banner section when unsupported-language units are present
Language detail rows show Grade: N/A for unsupported languages instead of a misleading letter grade
Aggregate score excludes unsupported languages so overall grade is not pulled down by N/A units
